application.desc: GROBID - A machine learning software for extracting information from scholarly documents

keywords.term: Ricinus communis 1.4.2.1.0 Cauliflower Brassica oleracea var. botrytis 1.5.5.1 Celeriac Apium graveolens var. rapaceum 1.5.3.8 Celery Apium graveolens 1.5.1.0.5 Chayote Sechium edule 1.5.2.1.2 Cherry (all varieties) Prunus spp. 2.1.3.2 Chestnut Castanea sativa 2.1.7.4 Chickpea (gram pea) Cicer arietinum 1.3.4 Chicory Cichorium intybus 1.4.5.2 Chicory for greens Cichorium intybus 1.5.1.0.6 Chili

fresh (all varieties) Capsicum spp. (annuum) 1.5.2.0.2 Cinnamon Cinnamomum verum 2.2.3.2 Citron Citrus medica 2.1.1.6 Citronella Cymbopogon citrates/ Cymbopogon nardus 2.2.7.1 Clementine Citrus reticulata 2.1.1.2 Clove Eugenia aromatica (Syzygium aromaticum) 2.2.3.3 Clover for fodder (all varieties) Trifolium spp. 1.7.1.2 Clover for seed (all varieties) Trifolium spp. 1.8.3 Cocoa (cacao) Theobroma cacao 2.2.1.2 Coconut Cocos nucifera 2.2.2.1 Cocoyam Colocasia esculenta 1.2.8 Coffee Coffea spp. 2.2.1.1 Cola nut (all varieties) Cola acuminata 2.2.1.6 Colza (rapeseed) Brassica napus 1.4.2.0.5 Corn (maize)

div.p: Musa textilis 2.2.4.1 Alfalfa for fodder Medicago sativa 1.7.1.1 Alfalfa for seed Medicago sativa 1.8.2 Almond Prunus dulcis 2.1.7.1 Anise seeds Pimpinella anisum 1.4.3.4 Apple Malus sylvestris 2.1.2.1 Apricot Prunus armeniaca 2.1.3.1 Areca (betel nut) Areca catechu 2.2.7.6 Arracha Arracacia xanthorrhiza 1.2.9 Arrowroot Maranta arundinacea 1.2.5 Artichoke Cynara scolymus 1.5.1.0.1 Asparagus Asparagus officinalis 1.5.1.0.2 Avocado Persea americana 2.1.6.0.1 World Programme for the Census of Agriculture 2010 Crop name Botanical name ICC code Previous code 1
